200.a: Elf
Shadowfax

200.q: What is the home of the hobbits called?
200.a: The Shire

200.q: What creature does Gandalf fight in Moria?
200.a: Balrog

200.q: What land do Frodo and Bilbo sail to at the end?
200.a: The Undying Lands

200.q: What creature bites off Frodo's finger?
200.a: Gollum

200.q: What forest is home to the Ents?
200.a: Fangorn Forest

200.q: What inn do the hobbits reach in Bree?
200.a: The Prancing Pony

200.q: What creature calls the Ring 'my precious'?
200.a: Gollum

200.q: What race are Frodo and Sam?
200.a: Hobbits

200.q: Who is the ring-bearer of the One Ring?
200.a: Frodo Baggins
Gollum — he bites it from Frodo and falls into Mount Doom

200.q: Who is the Dark Lord of Mordor?
200.a: Sauron
Sting — it glows blue when Orcs are near
A Stoor Hobbit originally named Sméagol
Shadowfax

200.q: How many members in the Fellowship?
200.a: Nine
Heir of Isildur — rightful King of Gondor
An Elf of Mirkwood — an expert archer and member of the Fellowship

200.q: What is the One Ring's famous inscription?
200.a: One Ring to rule them all — in the Black Speech of Mordor
Frodo's loyal gardener and closest friend — he carries Frodo up Mount Doom
The peaceful homeland of Hobbits in northwest Middle-earth
Son of the Steward of Gondor — he tries to take the Ring but dies protecting Merry and Pippin
The White Wizard who betrays the order and allies with Sauron
The shieldmaiden of Rohan who kills the Witch-King — No living man am I!
The kingdom of horse-lords — allied with Gondor, ruled by King Théoden
Frodo's uncle who found the Ring during The Hobbit — he carries it for 60 years
The vast forest kingdom of the Elves under King Thranduil
The oldest Ent — he leads the Ents to destroy Isengard

200.q: What is the significance of the Grey Havens?
200.a: The port where Elves sail to the Undying Lands — where Frodo departs at the end
Boromir's younger brother — he shows wisdom by not taking the Ring

400.q: What creature attacks Frodo near Cirith Ungol?
What is Aragorn's reforged sword called?
Who is the steward of Gondor during Return of the King?
What is Aragorn's reforged sword called?
What glowing gift helps Frodo against Shelob?
Who is the Steward of Gondor during the war?
What city does Gondor defend in Return of the King?
What race is Gimli?
What mountain pass does the Fellowship fail to cross before Moria?
What river separates the Fellowship before Boromir's fall?
What rider race serves Rohan?
What city do Frodo and Sam try to reach inside Mordor?
What title does Galadriel hold in Lothlorien?
What creature attacks the Fellowship outside Moria's gate?
Who is Frodo's most loyal companion?
Who is the wizard who sends Frodo on his quest?
What is the name of the dwarf in the Fellowship?
What is the name of the elf archer in the Fellowship?
Who is Legolas's father?
400.a: Thranduil, King of the Woodland Realm of Mirkwood

400.q: How many Ringwraiths are there?
What is Gandalf's sword called?
What are Ents?
400.a: Ancient tree shepherds — Treebeard is the oldest living creature in Middle-earth

400.q: What is Anduril?
400.a: The sword reforged from the shards of Narsil — Aragorn's weapon

400.q: How many Rings of Power total?
400.a: 20: 3 for Elves, 7 for Dwarves, 9 for Men, and the One Ring

400.q: What are the Dead Men of Dunharrow?
400.a: An army of oathbreakers — Aragorn commands them to fulfill their oath and destroy Sauron's fleet

400.q: What is Shelob?
400.a: An ancient giant spider in Cirith Ungol — she stings Frodo, apparently killing him

400.q: What is the Mines of Moria?
400.a: An ancient Dwarf realm — now full of Orcs and the Balrog — the Fellowship passes through

400.q: Who is Denethor?
400.a: The Steward of Gondor — he goes mad with grief, ordering Faramir to certain death

400.q: What is the Balrog?
400.a: An ancient fire demon from the Elder Days — Gandalf fights it on the Bridge of Khazad-dûm

400.q: What is Rivendell?
400.a: The Elvish refuge of Elrond — where the Fellowship is formed

400.q: What are the Palantíri?
400.a: Seeing stones allowing distant communication — Sauron corrupted Saruman and Denethor through them

400.q: Who is Galadriel?
400.a: The Lady of Lothlórien — one of the most powerful Elves, who resists the temptation of the Ring

400.q: What is the significance of Sam carrying Frodo?
400.a: At Mount Doom, Frodo can no longer walk — Sam carries him up in one of literature's greatest acts of friendship

400.q: What is the War of the Last Alliance?
400.a: Ancient war shown at the start of Fellowship — Elves and Men united, resulting in Isildur cutting off Sauron's finger

400.q: What is the significance of Boromir's death?
400.a: He dies protecting Merry and Pippin — My brother... my captain... my king — a powerful redemption

600.q: What are the two towers usually understood to be in The Two Towers?
What glowing gift does Galadriel give Frodo?
What does the word mithril refer to?
600.a: A rare, incredibly light and strong metal

600.q: What alias does Aragorn use in Bree?
What city do the Ring-bearers leave from to sail west?
What creature attacks the Fellowship outside Moria's doors?
What mountain must the Ring be destroyed in?
What light does Galadriel give Frodo?
What is Saruman's tower called?
What forest do the Ents live in?
What king of Rohan is under Saruman's influence?
What signal lights call Gondor for aid?
What is the name of Aragorn's horse?
What tower holds Sauron's eye?
What mountain range must the Fellowship cross before Moria?
What event do Treebeard and the ents launch against Isengard?
600.a: The Last March of the Ents

600.q: What elven bread do the travelers carry?
What is the original name of Gollum?
Where was the One Ring forged?
What is the name of Aragorn's sword reforged?
What is Bilbo's original name for his elven blade?
What Ent leads the assault on Isengard?
What is the name of Gandalf's sword?
Who is the Witch-king of Angmar's greatest fear?
600.a: No man can kill him (killed by Éowyn)

600.q: Who is Tom Bombadil?
600.a: A mysterious ancient being in the Old Forest unaffected by the Ring — his nature is never explained

600.q: What language inspired Tolkien's Quenya Elvish?
600.a: Finnish (Quenya); Sindarin was inspired by Welsh

600.q: What is the Silmarillion?
600.a: Tolkien's mythological history covering the creation of the world through all ages

600.q: Who are the Valar?
600.a: God-like beings who shaped Middle-earth — the highest power below Ilúvatar (the creator)

600.q: What is Morgoth?
600.a: The first Dark Lord — Sauron was originally his lieutenant. Morgoth was cast into the Void at the end of the First Age

600.q: What is the significance of Isildur keeping the Ring?
600.a: Rather than destroying it as Elrond urges, Isildur keeps it — setting in motion 3,000 years of catastrophe

600.q: What is the Akallabêth?
600.a: The tale of the downfall of Númenor — a great island kingdom swallowed by the sea after rebelling against the Valar

600.q: What is the significance of Gandalf being a Maia?
600.a: He is an angelic being sent to guide and inspire — not dominate. His restrictions explain why he doesn't simply overpower evil

600.q: What is the One Ring's fundamental nature?
600.a: It is an extension of Sauron's will — it seeks to return to its master and corrupts its bearers with desire for power
